page: Microsoft Azure E32-8ads_v5 is an EADSv5-family virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and 2 threads per core. The processor operates at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz and includes 32 KB L1d, 0.5 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding 32.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Storage consists of a bundled 1229 GB local disk, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. No GPU resources are included.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU capabilities, with stress-ng single-core performance ranking in the top 25%. However, multi-core CPU performance and overall computational tests, such as PassMark and Geekbench, fall within the average range. A notable hardware tradeoff is observed in memory bandwidth; while small-block cached read bandwidth is average at 515.77 GB/sec, large-block uncached read and write bandwidths are weak, scoring in the bottom 25% at 38.45 GB/sec and 22.42 GB/sec respectively. Large-model LLM inference also shows limitations, with prompt processing for a medium 7B model falling into the bottom 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this server offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio and bundled local storage, making it a specialized option for memory-bound workloads rather than raw compute-heavy tasks. The resource density is optimized for large in-memory databases, caching layers, and data buffering. Appropriate workloads include database operations, Redis deployments, and memory-intensive enterprise applications that benefit from the 256 GB RAM allocation and local SSD storage but do not require high-throughput uncached memory bandwidth or dense multi-core scaling.
description: Microsoft Azure E32-8ads_v5 is an x86_64 virtual machine in the EADSv5 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and an exceptionally high memory capacity of 256.0 GB, alongside a bundled 1229 GB local disk. While single-core CPU performance is strong, ranking in the top 25% in stress-ng tests, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks are weaker, falling into the bottom 25%. This resource profile represents a qualitative tradeoff, prioritizing massive memory density over high-throughput memory bus speeds. Consequently, this server is best suited for memory-intensive workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers like Redis, and data buffering applications that require substantial RAM capacity relative to compute resources.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32-8ads_v5 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB of RAM, and a 1229 GB local disk, optimized for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E32-8ads_v5 is an AMD-powered VM with 8 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 1229 GB local storage, designed for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: An AMD-powered virtual machine offering massive memory density and bundled local storage for memory-bound database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 4 physical cores
256 GB of system memory providing high RAM-to-vCPU density
Bundled 1229 GB local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong single-core CPU performance but weaker uncached memory bandwidth
Well-suited for in-memory databases, Redis caching, and memory-bound applications
